name: Trigger Alpert
abstract: Herman "Trigger" Alpert (September 3, 1916 – December 21, 2013) was an American jazz bassist from Indianapolis, Indiana.

about_quotes.speaker: Don Ewell
about_quotes.evidence: a fellow named Trigger Alpert a bass player with Glenn Miller yeah I've heard uh wanted a piano player and he was up in Indianapolis Fort Benjamin Harrison and uh they recommended me and he came down and I played one chorus of Sweet Lorraine and that seemed to satisfy him so so he uh arranged for my

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Flanigan
about_quotes.evidence: I do a piece called “Trigger Fantasy” with them which was written by Trigger Alpert — he lives in Punta Gorda Florida now I think
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Flanigan interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: John Best
about_quotes.evidence: The drummer was Maurice Purtill, the bass player was Rolly Bundock. He was replaced by Trigger Alpert.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Best interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Sauter
about_quotes.evidence: Trigger Alpert (phonetic spelling) had been with Bill Miller's band.
